stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The human body uses vitamin C to maintain healthy connective tissues.
  • Vitamin C is a nutrient found in many fresh foods.
  • Oranges contain 70 milligrams per 100 grams (mg/100g) of vitamin C.
  • Red bell peppers contain 128 mg/100g.
  • Guavas contain 228 mg/100g.
stem: The student wants to identify which of the three foods has the highest vitamin C content. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: At 128 mg/100g, red bell peppers contain more vitamin C than oranges (70 mg/100g).
Vitamin C is present in many fresh foods, including oranges, red bell peppers, and guavas.
Oranges contain 70 mg/100g of vitamin C, a nutrient used by the human body to maintain healthy connective tissues.
Guavas contain 228 mg/100g of vitamin C, which is more than oranges and red bell peppers.

rationale: Choice D is the best answer because it identifies guavas as having the highest content and supports the claim by noting their value exceeds both other foods. Choice A is incorrect because it compares only two of the three foods. Choice B is incorrect because it lists the foods without giving a ranking. Choice C is incorrect because it gives the content of a single food without comparison.
